content_markdown: The frequency of the first harmonic standing wave in a pipe that is open at both ends is $200\text{ Hz}$. What is the frequency of the first harmonic in a pipe of the same length that is open at one end and closed at the other?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: For a pipe open at both ends, the fundamental frequency is $f_{open} = \frac{v}{2L} = 200\text{ Hz}$. For a pipe of the same length $L$ closed at one end, the fundamental frequency is $f_{closed} = \frac{v}{4L}$. Since $f_{closed} = \frac{1}{2} f_{open}$, the new frequency is $100\text{ Hz}$.
